data.description:
## Why

Audit OSS finding #5 (MEDIUM). The docs tree has exceptional depth (36 top-level docs + 30-plus ADRs) but no navigation: the Documentation URL in pyproject lands on a bare directory listing, and the content skews heavily toward LLM-agent operators (CLAUDE.md is the richest usage doc but is agent-facing). A human newcomer gets the README Quickstart and then a wall of internals (plan-review-gate.md, workflow-authoring-v2.md). Two artifacts are missing: an index, and a human-oriented "using rebar day-to-day" guide.

## What

1. docs/README.md: a short index grouping every doc by audience — user / operator / contributor / agent — with one-line descriptions; link it from the main README and point pyproject's Documentation URL at it.
2. docs/user-guide.md: a human-facing guide distilling the CLI workflow (create/claim/comment/link/transition, search/list/ready, session logs, the gates as a user experiences them) without agent-protocol framing; cross-link rather than duplicate the deep docs.

## Scope

In: the two new docs, README + pyproject link updates, one-line description sweep of existing docs for the index. Out: rewriting existing docs, restructuring docs/ directories, changes to CLAUDE.md.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] docs/README.md exists, lists every file in docs/ (and the adr/ + design/ dirs as groups) under an audience heading with a one-line description
- [ ] docs/user-guide.md covers the day-to-day CLI loop end-to-end for a human reader, with working commands verified against the current build
- [ ] Main README links the index; pyproject [project.urls] Documentation points at docs/README.md
- [ ] No orphan docs: everything in docs/ is reachable from the index
data.title: Human-facing docs: docs/README.md index grouped by audience + a day-to-day user guide
